stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of water flow rate using a flow meter. The measurements have a mean of 190.6 L/min and a standard deviation of 23.4 L/min. However, calibration tests reveal that a flow meter systematically overestimates the true value by between 4.5% and 7.1%. Estimate the true water flow rate and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 190.6 L/min. After correcting for the 5.8% bias: ≈ 180.2 L/min. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.5%-7.1%) and sampling variability: [168.6, 191.8]. Point estimate: 180.2 L/min.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
